name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers same-day payday loans ($100–$500), installment loans up to $1,000, and title loans up to $25,000 at physical and online locations.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized lender specializing in short-term emergency cash solutions for consumers in financial need between paychecks. The company operates physical retail locations alongside online lending services, with a significant presence across multiple states including Florida. Their Jacksonville location at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave. exemplifies their brick-and-mortar accessibility, staffed by trained personnel available during extended weekday hours (10am–6pm) and weekend service on Saturdays.

Advance America's core product suite includes three primary loan types: Payday Loans ($100–$500, 2–4 week terms), Installment Loans ($100–$1,000 with flexible repayment schedules), and Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000 based on vehicle equity, offered in partnership with LoanCenter). All products are available through in-store application, online application, or phone pre-qualification. The company also offers Western Union money transfer services. Loan approval and same-day funding are marketed as core competitive advantages, with minimal documentation requirements (government ID, proof of income, bank account, Social Security Number/ITIN).

Advance America distinguishes itself through multi-channel accessibility (physical stores plus online/phone options), bilingual customer service ("Hablamos español"), and a stated reputation as a "fully accredited company." The Jacksonville store carries a 4.9-star rating across 125,454 reviews, with customer testimonials emphasizing staff helpfulness and process clarity. The company actively promotes customer referral rewards and maintains transparent loan term disclosures on their website.

Critical caveats apply: Payday and installment loans carry unspecified but typically very high APR rates (not disclosed on this location page), making them expensive short-term debt. Title loans require surrendering vehicle titles as collateral, creating repossession risk. These products are designed for financial emergencies, not long-term solutions, and carry predictable risks of debt cycling for vulnerable consumers. Advance America's business model is predicated on short-term, high-margin lending to credit-constrained populations.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding available for payday and installment loans
High loan approval rates with minimal credit requirements
Multiple application channels: in-store, online, and phone pre-qualification
Extended business hours (10am–6pm weekdays, 9am–4pm Saturday) for convenience
Bilingual staff and customer service (Spanish-language support stated)
Higher loan limits with installment loans ($1,000) versus payday loans ($500)
Title loans up to $25,000 for consumers with vehicle equity
4.9-star customer rating based on 125,454 reviews with positive testimonials about staff helpfulness
cons: APR rates not disclosed on location page; payday/installment loans typically carry 300%+ APR (industry standard), making them extremely expensive
Short repayment terms (2–4 weeks for payday loans) create high default and debt-cycling risk
Title loans require surrendering vehicle title as collateral, risking repossession if payment missed
Designed for emergency short-term use, not suitable for long-term debt solutions or building credit
No mention of credit-building or credit reporting benefits; loans do not improve credit scores
best_for: Workers needing bridge cash before payday with reliable income to repay within 2–4 weeks
Vehicle owners with equity seeking larger emergency loans ($2,000–$25,000) and ability to repay without risking car repossession
Consumers in Jacksonville's North Edgewood area preferring in-person application and immediate cash access
Spanish-language speakers seeking bilingual lending services in Jacksonville
diagnosis: Advance America is best for workers facing legitimate cash emergencies who can repay within 2–4 weeks and prefer immediate in-person service. The primary caveat: these loans carry triple-digit APRs and should be treated as last-resort emergency funding, not regular borrowing solutions; consumers risk debt cycles or vehicle repossession without careful evaluation of repayment capacity.
services: Payday loans ($100–$500, 2–4 week terms, online or in-store)
Installment loans ($100–$1,000, multiple payment options, online or in-store)
Title loans ($2,000–$25,000, vehicle collateral-based, in partnership with LoanCenter)
In-store application and loan closing at physical locations
Loan application pre-visit guidance and required documentation list

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
